module_info.docstring:
Dangerous command approval -- detection, prompting, and per-session state.

This module is the single source of truth for the dangerous command system:
- Pattern detection (DANGEROUS_PATTERNS, detect_dangerous_command)
- Per-session approval state (thread-safe, keyed by session_key)
- Approval prompting (CLI interactive + gateway async)
- Permanent allowlist persistence (config.yaml)

functions.name: detect_dangerous_command
functions.docstring:
Check if a command matches any dangerous patterns.

Returns:
    (is_dangerous, pattern_key, description) or (False, None, None)

functions.name: submit_pending
functions.docstring: Store a pending approval request for a session.

functions.name: pop_pending
functions.docstring: Retrieve and remove a pending approval for a session.

functions.name: has_pending
functions.docstring: Check if a session has a pending approval request.

functions.name: approve_session
functions.docstring: Approve a pattern for this session only.

functions.name: is_approved
functions.docstring: Check if a pattern is approved (session-scoped or permanent).

functions.name: approve_permanent
functions.docstring: Add a pattern to the permanent allowlist.

functions.name: load_permanent
functions.docstring: Bulk-load permanent allowlist entries from config.

functions.name: clear_session
functions.docstring: Clear all approvals and pending requests for a session.

functions.name: load_permanent_allowlist
functions.docstring:
Load permanently allowed command patterns from config.

Also syncs them into the approval module so is_approved() works for
patterns added via 'always' in a previous session.

functions.name: save_permanent_allowlist
functions.docstring: Save permanently allowed command patterns to config.

functions.name: prompt_dangerous_approval
functions.docstring:
Prompt the user to approve a dangerous command (CLI only).

Args:
    approval_callback: Optional callback registered by the CLI for
        prompt_toolkit integration. Signature: (command, description) -> str.

Returns: 'once', 'session', 'always', or 'deny'

functions.name: check_dangerous_command
functions.docstring:
Check if a command is dangerous and handle approval.

This is the main entry point called by terminal_tool before executing
any command. It orchestrates detection, session checks, and prompting.

Args:
    command: The shell command to check.
    env_type: Terminal backend type ('local', 'ssh', 'docker', etc.).
    approval_callback: Optional CLI callback for interactive prompts.

Returns:
    {"approved": True/False, "message": str or None, ...}
